clause_text: This agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Delaware, United States of America, without regard to its conflict of law provisions.
risk_reason: Specifying US state law as governing law is unlikely to be upheld by Saudi courts for agreements executed and substantially performed in the Kingdom. Creates uncertainty for the Saudi contracting party.
saudi_legal_note: وفق نظام المعاملات المدنية السعودي، تُطبَّق أحكام القانون السعودي على العقود المنفَّذة داخل المملكة بصرف النظر عن بند اختيار القانون. اختيار قانون أجنبي لا يُلغي الأحكام الآمرة في النظام السعودي.
recommended_revision: Replace with: This agreement shall be governed by the laws of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia. Any dispute shall be referred to the competent Saudi Commercial Court or resolved by arbitration in Riyadh under SCCA rules.
related_regulation: Saudi Civil Transactions Law (Royal Decree M/191 1443H) Art. 22 | Saudi Commercial Courts Law (Royal Decree M/93 1441H)
notes: Common boilerplate in US-origin SaaS agreements that conflicts with Saudi legal practice — flag in every contract review

clause_text: All intellectual property rights in the platform and in any customisations, integrations, or derivative works developed under this agreement shall vest exclusively in and remain the property of the Service Provider, regardless of which party funded their development.
risk_reason: Client-funded customisations assigned entirely to vendor without compensation or licence-back creates significant commercial imbalance and may conflict with commissioned work principles under Saudi civil law.
saudi_legal_note: نظام المعاملات المدنية السعودي يُجيز الاتفاق على ملكية الأعمال المُنجزة بموجب العقد، لكن الأعمال المُموَّلة كليًا من العميل تستوجب النظر في توزيع ملكية عادل أو منح ترخيص دائم للعميل. يُوصى بالتمييز بين ملكية المنصة الأساسية وملكية الأعمال المُخصَّصة.
recommended_revision: Negotiate: (1) vendor retains ownership of core platform IP, (2) client-funded bespoke customisations vest in the client or are subject to a perpetual royalty-free licence, (3) each party grants the other a licence to use their background IP solely to perform this agreement.
related_regulation: Saudi Civil Transactions Law (Royal Decree M/191 1443H) | Intellectual Property Protection Law (Royal Decree M/41 1424H)
notes: TO VERIFY — exact treatment of commissioned software IP under Saudi civil law. Seek specialist IP counsel for high-value customisation projects.
